name: Chico O'Farrill
abstract: Arturo "Chico" O'Farrill (October 28, 1921 – June 27, 2001) was a Cuban composer, arranger, and conductor, best known for his work in the Latin idiom, specifically Afro-Cuban jazz or "Cubop", although he also composed traditional jazz pieces and even symphonic works. Born to an aristocratic Cuban family, he played the trumpet early in hi…

about_quotes.speaker: Victor Rendon
about_quotes.evidence: Chico O’Farrill wrote four of the arrangements for that. It was two big band sessions — one big band with two tunes, and then a smaller group, four horn things that he did with us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Victor Rendon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Dodgion
about_quotes.evidence: I got a call for three days of recording with Chico O’Farrill on the answering service. And I showed up and it was Count Basie’s band... I even played a few solos on that
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Paquito D'Rivera
about_quotes.evidence: O’Farrill had a lot of work, and he share the work with me. Also Chico O’Farrill, give me some gigs in the jingle industry.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paquito D'Rivera — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Vincent Pelote
about_quotes.evidence: And he said he did some work for Benny Goodman — oh no, he said he was approached by Benny Goodman to do arrangements for the big band but he turned it down and gave it to Chico O’Farrill. Now I’ve heard Chico O’Farrill talk about that, and not once did he ever mention Gil Fuller giving him that job
about_quotes.doc_title: Vincent Pelote interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 25, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Vega
about_quotes.evidence: Chico O’Farrill conducted it and we would run it... we’d run it one time... And he’d go, “Okay let’s have a run through. One-two — ” and we’d run it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Vega interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 20, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: The one with Chico was . . . it was called Drum Fever. Well, that was with a big orchestra, huge, huge.
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
